title: leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. cuneifolia
intro: Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. cuneifolia, also known as Alpine Cushion Daisy, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. cuneifolia include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use is mostly limited to traditional medicine due to its rarity and conservation status.
